title: Path: discovery cascade — iron + coal + steel + modern weapons tiers
description:
Per todo.org §Path. Long chain of unlocks gated on what you've discovered in the wilds. Combined into one bead because each unlock is small but they share the "discover X → unlock Y, Z" pattern.

## Scope

### Iron tier (on iron / iron-mine discovery)
- Cask, wagon, i armor, iron sword in workshop.
- Iron in buy menu.
- Wandering Scout event.
- Scout ability (reveals adjacent map squares?).

### Coal + medicine tier (on coal / coal-mine / medicine discovery)
- Coal in buy menu.
- Steelworks unlock.
- Coal-miner / iron-miner / steelworker villager roles.
- Plague event (medicine-gated).
- 'Use meds' option.
- Meds in buy menu.
- Steel additions: water tank, convoy, s armor, steel sword.

### Modern weapons tier (on rifle / bullets / bolas / energy-cell / alien-alloy / grenades / bayonet discovery)
- Rifle → shoot attack + bullets buy.
- Bolas → tangle attack + bolas buy.
- Energy cell → unlock energy weapons (TBD by polecat — match original game).
- Alien alloy → buyable.
- Grenades → 'lob' attack + buy.
- Bayonet → buy + new attack.
- Armory → bullet-making villager.
- Crashed Ship discovery → unlock 'An Old Starship' marker on map.

## Out of scope
- An Old Starship endgame content (separate, design-needed bead).
- Existing place rewards (handled by Places bead).

## Verify
- `cabal build` clean.
- Manual: discover iron + coal + at least one modern thing → see all corresponding unlocks (workshop items, buy menu items, attacks, villagers, events).

## Reference
- todo.org §Path
notes: Implemented discovery cascade: iron/coal/medicine/modern weapon tiers gate workshop crafts, buy menu, villager roles, wandering scout + plague events, use meds, and combat specials (shoot/tangle/lob/bayonet). Clearing Crashed Ship reveals the An Old Starship menu. Build clean (cabal build), no new warnings.

title: Path: 12 explorable places + post-exploration outpost markers + roads between outposts
description:
Per todo.org §Path Create-explorable-places. The dozen places, the 'P' outpost marker, the roads connecting them, and inventory persistence across expeditions.

## Scope
- 12 places (each is a multi-room mini-dungeon with loot + sometimes enemies):
  - A Damp Cave
  - The Iron Mine
  - Outposts
  - An Old House
  - The Coal Mine
  - A Deserted Town
  - The Sulfur Mine
  - A Forgotten Battlefield
  - A Ruined City
  - A Huge Borehole
  - A Crashed Ship
  - A Murky Swamp
- After exploration: places become outpost 'P' markers (excluding Iron Mines per todo.org).
- Roads/paths render between explored outposts on the map.
- Going home maintains inventory across expeditions (e.g., found a sword instead of a spear → next time you take the sword).

## Out of scope
- Discovery cascade unlocks (iron tier / coal tier / modern tier — separate bead).

## Verify
- `cabal build` clean.
- Manual: walk to ≥3 different places, explore, leave; see them as 'P', roads connect, inventory persists.

## Reference
- todo.org §Path Create-explorable-places
notes: Implemented: 12 explorable places (Shared.Place + Path.Place scene scripts), outpost glyph conversion ('P', iron mine keeps 'I'), L-shaped roads from village to each cleared place, inventory persistence via pathPreferredAllocation -> preallocate on next arrival. New modules: Shared.Place, Shared.PathMap, Path.Place. Build clean (cabal build), no new warnings.
close_reason: Merged to master as 454dc42. Build clean.

title: Path: wandering attack events catalog (gaunt man, scavenger, meat eater, feral terror, soldier, sniper)
description:
Per todo.org §Path Attack-wandering-events. Add the six other wandering attack types beyond the snarling beast; reuses the combat system.

## Scope
Each enemy type (verify the snarling beast is already in via Combat bead):
- a gaunt man
- a scavenger
- a meat eater
- a feral terror
- a soldier
- a sniper

Per type:
- Random spawn rate while walking (match original game).
- Uses the Combat bead's parameterized system (HP, damage, drop table).
- Drops items appropriate to type (e.g., scavenger → scrap, soldier → bullets).

## Out of scope
- Combat system itself (separate bead).
- Place encounters.

## Verify
- `cabal build` clean.
- Manual: walk around long enough to encounter at least 3 distinct types; each fights correctly + drops appropriate loot.

## Reference
- todo.org §Path
notes: Implemented: Path.Enemy catalog with 7 wandering-attack types (snarling beast, gaunt man, scavenger, meat eater, feral terror, soldier, sniper). Each has name, HP, damage, hit chance, attack delay, rolls/chance drop table, and spawn weight; numbers track original A Dark Room balance. Drops scale with type (scavenger has Iron/Leather, soldier/sniper have Rifle/Bullets/Steel, etc). Pure data module — combat system (ad-y3y) consumes it. cabal build clean, no new warnings. Note: end-to-end manual verification (walking, combat) requires Combat (ad-y3y) and Path-movement (ad-1es) beads to land — this bead is the data dependency.

title: Path: reusable rewards screen (post-combat + post-exploration)
description:
Per todo.org §Path reusable-rewards-screen. View used after combat AND exploration.

## Scope
- Reusable view taking:
  - Variable text ("You killed the beast.").
  - List of takeable items + amounts.
  - List of droppable items.
  - 'Take all' button (takes as many as fit).
  - 'Take all you can' button.
  - 'Eat meat' button (heals).
  - 'Continue' / 'Leave' buttons (context-dependent).
- Wire into Combat bead so beast death → rewards.

## Out of scope
- Place encounters wiring (handled in Places bead).

## Verify
- `cabal build` clean.
- Manual: kill beast → rewards screen → all buttons functional.

## Reference
- todo.org §Path
notes: Implemented: Reusable Rewards modal — variable text, takeable items with [+] buttons, droppable items with [-] buttons, take-all (takes as many as fit), eat-meat (heals via CuredMeat), context-row (Combat: eat-meat+leave; Exploration: continue+leave). Modal blocks bottom-menu actions via existing actionButton plumbing. Combat-side wiring deferred — no Combat module yet; debug button 'rw.' in bottom menu opens modal for manual verification. Resumed nux's commit aafbd70 cleanly on master; cabal build clean.

title: Path: combat system (snarling beast as canonical encounter; reusable for other wandering events)
description:
Per todo.org §Path snarling-beast-attack-event. Build the combat system — random encounter while walking, two characters, attack, heal, death paths. Other wandering events (Bead "wandering events catalog") will reuse this.

## Scope
- Snarling beast random encounter while walking.
- Display two characters (player, beast).
- Animate both on attack.
- Attack button does damage to beast.
- Cured meat consumable heals player HP.
- Beast dies → trigger rewards screen (separate bead).
- Player dies → blackout (uses Movement bead's blackout).
- Combat-system abstractions parameterizable so other wandering events can reuse (HP, attack damage, drop table).

## Out of scope
- Rewards screen UI (separate bead).
- Other wandering events (separate bead).
- Place encounters (separate bead).

## Verify
- `cabal build` clean.
- Manual: encounter beast → fight → win triggers rewards (placeholder OK if rewards bead hasn't merged yet); lose → blackout.

## Reference
- todo.org §Path
notes: Resolved rebase conflicts onto master (2e59f9a) in cabal/Path.hs/UI/Display.hs. Combat commit replayed cleanly: BeastSpec/Combat state, Path.triggerBeastFight, drawCombatWindow stack, manual fight button on path map. Build clean.

metadata.rejection_reason: Conflicts with master at 2e59f9a16b55ee2cbbb5d67601259cf1a6409695 in: adarkroom-port.cabal, app/Path.hs, app/UI/Display.hs

title: Path: character movement, food/water consumption, blackout state
description:
Per todo.org §Path. Movement input + food/water mechanics + blackout.

## Scope
- Character movement on the map (use the brick TUI's existing input style — likely hjkl or arrows).
- 'A' key → return home (back to Path-unlocked screen, preserving inventory).
- Walking displays messages when entering new areas.
- Walking removes water (per N steps; match original game).
- Walking removes food (cured meat) (per N steps).
- Out-of-water → end exploration → blackout.
- Out-of-food → end exploration → blackout.
- Blackout state: cooldown before next exploration; on entry, undo this expedition (gathered loot, explored squares).

## Out of scope
- Combat / wandering events / places.

## Verify
- `cabal build` clean.
- Manual: walk, see water/food deplete; deplete to 0 → blackout → cooldown → recover.

## Reference
- todo.org §Path
notes: Resumed after rejection. Resolved app/UI/Display.hs conflict against master (16156cd PrePath: trading post buy menu): kept drawRewardsWindow in the drawUI overlay stack and dropped the orphaned dummyMap (already moved to Path.pathMapData in this branch). cabal build clean.

title: Path: rucksack box (HP, free space, items) + path map (fog of war + @ marker)
description:
Per todo.org §Path. Render rucksack info + the map.

## Scope
- Rucksack box on Path screen:
  - Current HP.
  - Free / total rucksack space.
  - List of items + amounts.
- Path map:
  - Render full map (debug-only, then conditionally).
  - Fog of war — only show seen area.
  - Squares around character become 'seen'.
  - Character location as `@`.

## Out of scope
- Movement input (separate bead).
- Combat / places.

## Verify
- `cabal build` clean.
- Manual: embark → rucksack visible with stats + items; map shows only seen area + @.

## Reference
- todo.org §Path
notes: Resumed after rejection. Rebased polecat/ad-t9i onto 188a182 (combat system). Conflict in app/UI/Display.hs: kept the new rucksackBox panel + renderPathMap fog-of-war and merged in master's StartBeastFightButton trigger inside drawPathMap. Path.hs already adopted master's pathPlayer/pathSeen/pathMapData with sightRadius=2 + seenAround helper. cabal build clean.

title: Path-unlocked: supply allocation screen + embark button
description:
Per todo.org §Path unlocked. Screen between dusty-path unlock and embarking. Allocate path supplies, see capacity, embark.

## Scope
- Path supply display window. todo.org explicitly says **don't add hover tooltips** — keep info on-page.
  - Show player armor.
  - Show water (from waterskin / water tank).
  - Show variable-path-supplies available to take (cured meat, torches, etc).
  - Show variable-path-supplies in current expedition inventory.
  - +/- buttons for variable-path-supplies allocation.
  - Show free-items count in rucksack.
- Embark button. Tapping it transitions to the Path screen (placeholder for now — actual map is the next phase).

## Out of scope
- Path screen / map / movement (next phase).

## Verify
- `cabal build` clean.
- Manual: dusty path unlocked → page shows correct supplies; +/- works; Embark transitions.

## Reference
- todo.org §Path unlocked
notes: Implemented: Path supply allocation screen + embark button. Adds Game._embarked + Game._expeditionInventory, new Path module with arrival/embark/increaseSupply/decreaseSupply, and drawPathSupplies UI showing armor/water/supplies (cured meat, torch) with +/- allocation, rucksack capacity display, and embark button that flips state to the existing placeholder map. Each visit to Path resets allocation. cabal build clean, no new warnings.

title: PrePath: workshop building + craft buttons (torch, waterskin, rucksack, l armor) + player stats + max-hut msg + bone-spear weapons + compass-unlocks-dusty-path
description:
Per todo.org PrePath. Workshop building enables crafting; player stats system tracks HP / water capacity / inventory / armor; crafted items modify stats; bone spear unlocks weapons category; compass unlocks "A Dusty Path"; show max-hut msg.

## Scope
- Workshop building (build button + post-build state).
- Craft buttons in workshop: torch, waterskin, rucksack, light armor.
- Internal player stats model: HP, water capacity, inventory capacity, armor.
- Effects: waterskin → +water capacity; rucksack → +inventory; light armor → +armor.
- Bone spear → unlock 'weapons' category in crafting.
- Compass equipped → 'A Dusty Path' becomes visible/embarkable (just the unlock; embark UI is the next bead).
- Display 'max huts' message at 10 huts (mirror existing 'max traps' pattern).

## Out of scope
- Path-unlocked screen UI (next bead).
- Anything past path unlock.

## Verify
- `cabal build` clean.
- Manual: build workshop, craft each item, see stats change; reach 10 huts → max msg; equip compass → dusty path appears.

## Reference
- todo.org §PrePath
notes: Refinery rejection fix (gastown.capable): target updated from 'main' to 'master' (origin only has 'master'). Branch unchanged; implementation complete on gc-gastown.rictus-49231ab3f080 (commits 0ab0114, 97f71d9).

metadata.rejection_reason: Target branch 'main' does not exist on origin (only 'master' is available). Update metadata.target to 'master' and reassign.

title: PrePath: trading post + buy menu (teeth, compass) + tannery + smokehouse + scales + Mysterious Wanderer
description:
Per todo.org PrePath. Trading post unlocks the buy menu (teeth, compass for now). Tannery unlocks tanners (leather). Smokehouse unlocks charcutiers (cured meat). Buy menu unlocks scales when prereqs met. Mysterious Wanderer event added.

## Scope
- Mysterious Wanderer random event (mirror Beast Attack pattern).
- Trading post building unlocks buy menu.
- Buy menu: teeth, compass.
- Tannery building → tanners villager role (leather).
- Smokehouse building → charcutiers villager role (cured meat).
- Buy menu: scales (unlocked via prereqs).
- Bottom panel updates to show tanner / charcutier amounts.

## Out of scope
- Workshop / crafting / player stats.
- More buy items beyond teeth + compass + scales.

## Verify
- `cabal build` clean.
- Manual: build trading post → buy menu present; build tannery + smokehouse → roles unlock + amounts visible; trigger Wanderer.

## Reference
- todo.org §PrePath
notes: Resumed after rebase rejection. Rebased polecat/ad-e5c onto master e91c11b; resolved app/Shared/UI.hs conflict by keeping both feature additions (BuyButton next to CraftButton; EmbarkButton + IncreaseSupplyButton + DecreaseSupplyButton in their own Path supply allocation group). Auto-merge handled app/Event.hs and app/UI/Display.hs cleanly. cabal build clean (pre-existing warnings only).

title: PrePath: hunting lodge unlocks hunters + trappers (with +/- + amount displays)
description:
Per todo.org PrePath. Hunting lodge building unlocks two villager roles: hunters (meat) and trappers (fur). Each has +/- buttons and shows production.

## Scope
- Build flow for "hunting lodge" (mirror existing buildings).
- After build: hunter + trapper roles in villager allocation UI.
- +/- buttons reallocate villagers between gatherer ↔ hunter and gatherer ↔ trapper.
- Display hunting + trapping amounts (extend the gatherers bottom panel, or harmonize with whatever pattern landed).
- Match rates from the original web game.

## Out of scope
- Tannery / smokehouse / workshop.

## Verify
- `cabal build` clean.
- Manual: build lodge, allocate hunter/trapper, amounts show, +/- works.

## Reference
- todo.org §PrePath
notes: Re-rebased onto current origin/master (16156cd). Resolved conflicts: Shared/UI.hs (kept both rewards buttons + IncWorker/DecWorker), UI/Display.hs (kept ad-cuf's villagersWindow which already includes Tanner/Charcutier rows from master plus Hunter/Trapper +/- assignment). cabal build clean.
close_reason: Mayor cleanup: hunting lodge work landed at 1af0243 but bead lifecycle didn't close. Back-filling merge metadata + closing.

title: PrePath: villagers act as gatherers + always-on bottom display of gathering amounts
description:
Per todo.org PrePath. Villagers (population) act as gatherers, producing wood/fur/meat over time. Show an **always-on bottom display** of gathering amounts (todo.org explicitly notes a bottom display works better than the web game's hover tooltips).

## Scope
- Villager → gatherer role producing wood/fur/meat each tick.
- Bottom panel showing current gathering rates; updates every tick.
- Wire into the brick TUI's main layout.
- Match production rates from the original web game.

## Out of scope
- Hunters/trappers (separate bead).
- Building UI for hunting lodge / tannery / etc.

## Verify
- `cabal build` clean.
- Manual: time passes → resources tick up → bottom display shows the gather rate.

## Reference
- Original web game logic: https://github.com/doublespeakgames/adarkroom
- todo.org §PrePath
notes: Implemented worker income system: 10s WorkerIncome event applies workerReward per assigned worker; new villagers default to Gatherer; added always-on gathering panel showing per-second production rates. cabal build clean (ghc-9.4.2).

title: Packaging: Homebrew formula for brew install adarkroom-port
description:
## Goal
Ship a Homebrew formula so anyone on macOS/Linux can run:

```
brew tap jordangedney/tap
brew install adarkroom-port
adarkroom-port
```

## Scope
1. **Cut a release tag** on `https://github.com/jordangedney/adarkroom-port` (e.g. `v0.1.0` matching the .cabal `version: 0.1.0.0`). Homebrew formulas point at a tarball + sha256, so we need a fixed release artifact.
2. **Write `Formula/adarkroom-port.rb`** — Haskell formula targeting cabal:
   - `class AdarkroomPort < Formula`
   - `desc "Terminal port of A Dark Room (Haskell + brick)"`
   - `homepage "https://github.com/jordangedney/adarkroom-port"`
   - `url "https://github.com/jordangedney/adarkroom-port/archive/refs/tags/v0.1.0.tar.gz"` + `sha256 "..."`
   - `license "MPL-2.0"`
   - `depends_on "ghc" => :build`
   - `depends_on "cabal-install" => :build`
   - `install` block: `system "cabal", "v2-update"` then `system "cabal", "v2-install", "--installdir=#{bin}", "--install-method=copy", "--overwrite-policy=always"`
   - `test do` block: launch `adarkroom-port` with a tty harness and assert it exits cleanly on input — or, minimally, `assert_match "adarkroom", shell_output("#{bin}/adarkroom-port --help", 0)` if `--help` is wired (it may not be — fall back to `system "#{bin}/adarkroom-port", "--version"` if version flag exists; otherwise document the limitation).
3. **Decide tap location**:
   - Option A (recommended): create `jordangedney/homebrew-tap` repo, drop `Formula/adarkroom-port.rb` there. Trivial to maintain, users tap once.
   - Option B: submit to `homebrew/core` — slower review process, requires the formula meet Homebrew's criteria for inclusion (notability bar, etc.). Defer.
4. **Document install** in `README.md` (add a "Install via Homebrew" section).
5. **CI** (optional, P4): GitHub Actions on the main repo that runs `brew install --build-from-source ./Formula/adarkroom-port.rb` on a macOS runner to catch formula breakage on every push.

## Prerequisites
- GHC version compatibility: the .cabal targets GHC 9.10-era (per commit `97f71d9 fix build for ghc-9.10 / brick-2.10 / vty-6`). Homebrew's `ghc` formula tracks a recent GHC; verify it builds against whatever brew ships, and pin via `depends_on "ghc@9.10"` if needed.
- Confirm the binary runs without external runtime assets — if it needs data files, package via `pkgshare`.

## Verify
- `brew install jordangedney/tap/adarkroom-port` → succeeds on a clean macOS box.
- `adarkroom-port` launches; can play through the opening (light fire → gather wood → first villager).
- `brew test adarkroom-port` passes.
- `brew audit --strict --new --online adarkroom-port` reports zero issues.

## Out of scope
- homebrew/core submission (separate effort).
- Linuxbrew-specific tweaks (should "just work" but not tested).
- Pre-built bottles (let Homebrew build from source initially).
notes: Implemented: Homebrew formula at Formula/adarkroom-port.rb (cabal v2-install, ghc@9.10 build dep, placeholder sha256 with update instructions, HEAD fallback for pre-tag testing). Added CHANGELOG.md (referenced by cabal extra-doc-files) and README Install/Releasing sections. brew style passes cleanly. Out of scope: cutting v0.1.0 tag (requires master push), creating jordangedney/homebrew-tap, CI.

title: An Old Starship: endgame (NEEDS OVERSEER DESIGN BEFORE IMPLEMENTATION)
description:
Per todo.org §An-Old-Starship. Original todo.org reads: "Fuck me, how does this translate to terminal?"

## Status
**DO NOT CLAIM YET. NEEDS OVERSEER DESIGN.**

The web game's endgame is animation-heavy: lift-off sequence, alien battle, asteroid dodging, atmospheric reentry. Translating that to a brick TUI is a non-trivial design problem.

## Possible approaches (for design discussion)
- ASCII frame-by-frame animation.
- Scripted text-only sequence with key beats.
- Abstract turn-based encounter (asteroids as a series of dodge-rolls, alien battle as a hard combat encounter).
- Hybrid: text for narrative beats, abstract turn-based for action segments.

## When implementation begins
This bead will be split into sub-beads once the design is locked. Implementation TBD.

## Out of scope
- Anything not endgame.

## Reference
- todo.org §An Old Starship

title: mol-refinery-patrol
description:
Refinery patrol loop. Poured as a root-only wisp on startup:

  gc bd mol wisp mol-refinery-patrol --root-only --var target_branch=master --var rig_name=adarkroom-port --var binding_prefix=gastown.
  gc bd update $WISP --assignee=$GC_AGENT

Each wisp is ONE iteration: check for work, merge one branch, pour
the next iteration. On crash, re-read the formula steps and determine
where you left off from context (git state, bead state, last action).

Formula steps are NOT materialized as child beads. Read the step
descriptions below and work through them in order.

The loop mechanism: every exit path (happy or early) pours the next
wisp before burning this one. The prompt only bootstraps the first wisp.

Work beads flow directly: pool → polecat → refinery → closed.
No separate MR beads. The polecat sets metadata (branch, target) on
the work bead and assigns it to the refinery. On rejection, the
refinery puts the bead back in the pool with rejection metadata.

Merge strategy is per-work-bead metadata:
- `direct` (default): fast-forward merge to target and push
- `mr` / `pr`: publish a GitHub pull request instead of landing directly

In `mr` mode, refinery treats PR publication as the terminal handoff for
the direct-bead workflow: it records the PR URL on the work bead and
closes the bead once the PR is verified.

Read each step's description before acting — Config values override defaults.
